description: Evaluation test cases for skill-eval-generator covering triggering vs. deferral to the runner, refusal when the target skill is missing, browse-before-design grounding, FULL-mode fresh generation for the commit-message-writer fixture skill, INCREMENTAL merge that preserves human-refined content, [auto]-prefix and schema field-name discipline, generated-set coverage (negatives plus difficulty gradient), and eval-directory layout resolution.

evals.name: defer-running-evals-to-runner
evals.prompt: Run the existing evals for commit-message-writer and tell me the pass rate.
evals.expected_output: The skill should recognize this is an execution request, not a generation request, and defer to /skill-eval-runner rather than generating or regenerating evals.json.
expectations: Response recognizes the request is to run/execute evals, not to generate or scaffold them.
Response defers to /skill-eval-runner rather than writing or overwriting an evals.json.
Response does not fabricate pass rates, benchmark numbers, or grading results.

evals.name: refuse-generate-for-nonexistent-skill
evals.prompt: Generate evals for a skill called quantum-teleporter-9000. Scaffold the full evals.json now.
evals.expected_output: The skill should attempt to locate skills/quantum-teleporter-9000/SKILL.md, find it absent, and decline to generate evals for a skill that does not exist rather than inventing a testable surface area.
expectations: Response reports that the target skill quantum-teleporter-9000 cannot be found under skills/.
Response declines to generate evals.json for the non-existent skill instead of inventing scenarios.
Response does not fabricate a SKILL.md, references, routing tables, or expectations for a skill it never read.

evals.name: browse-before-design-grounds-in-skill
evals.prompt: Generate evals for the commit-message-writer skill at files/fixture-skills/commit-message-writer. Read the skill first and ground the eval scenarios in what it actually specifies.
evals.expected_output: A PHASE 1 browse of the fixture SKILL.md (and references/examples.md) followed by eval scenarios that reflect the skill's real content: the change-type routing table, the 50/72-character formatting thresholds, the breaking-change !/footer rule, and the When-NOT-to-use boundaries. Expectations should test conclusions, not incidental keywords.
expectations: Response reads the fixture SKILL.md before designing any eval scenarios.
Response derives at least one eval from the change-type routing table (e.g. feat vs fix vs docs selection).
Response derives at least one eval from a concrete threshold in the skill, such as the 50-character subject limit, 72-character body wrap, or the >1-concern body rule.
Response scans the referenced examples.md rather than relying only on the SKILL.md body.
Generated expectations test the correct conclusion (e.g. which type is chosen and why) rather than merely whether a keyword appears.

evals.name: full-mode-fresh-generation-valid-schema
evals.prompt: There are no evals yet for commit-message-writer (fixture at files/fixture-skills/commit-message-writer). Do a full generation pass and write a valid evals.json.
evals.expected_output: A FULL-mode generation: a valid evals.json whose skill_name is commit-message-writer, skill_version matches the fixture's metadata.version (2.1.0), IDs are unique and sequential, names are kebab-case, and the set includes a mix of eval types with at least one negative case.
expectations: Response produces a single valid JSON object with the required top-level fields skill_name, skill_version, and evals.
Response sets skill_name to commit-message-writer and skill_version to the fixture skill's metadata.version value 2.1.0.
Every generated eval entry has a unique integer id and a kebab-case name.
The generated set includes at least one negative or When-NOT-to-use eval (e.g. declining to actually run git commit, or refusing a no-changes request).
The generated set spans a difficulty gradient, with at least one clear-cut case and one ambiguous or multi-concern case.

evals.name: incremental-merge-preserves-human-refined
evals.prompt: An evals.json already exists for commit-message-writer (see files/fixture-existing-evals/mixed-provenance-evals.json). Regenerate evals for the fixture skill and merge with the existing file instead of overwriting it.
evals.expected_output: An INCREMENTAL merge that treats the existing file as authoritative for human content: expectations and prompts WITHOUT the [auto] prefix are preserved verbatim, [auto]-prefixed prompts/expectations may be replaced, the manually added eval (id 7, manual-multi-scope-monorepo) survives untouched, and any new eval entries get IDs starting from max(existing id)+1 so they never collide with id 7.
expectations: Response preserves the human-refined (non-[auto]) expectation on eval id 1 verbatim rather than rewriting or dropping it.
Response preserves the manually added eval id 7 (manual-multi-scope-monorepo) and its non-[auto] prompt and expectations unchanged.
Response may replace or refresh only the prompts and expectations that carry the [auto] prefix.
Response assigns any brand-new eval entries IDs starting from max(existing id)+1, i.e. 8 or higher, so they do not collide with the existing id 7.
Response does not create a second duplicate evals.json; it merges back into the file that already exists.

evals.name: auto-prefix-and-schema-field-names
evals.prompt: Generate evals for the commit-message-writer fixture and be strict about the output schema and the [auto]-prefix convention.
evals.expected_output: Output that respects the runner's schema exactly: the array key is expectations (not assertions), the per-variant field is expected_output, and the [auto] prefix appears on prompt and on each expectations entry but NOT on name, id, expected_output, skill_name, skill_version, or description.
expectations: Response uses the exact field name expectations for the checks array and does not rename it to assertions or checks.
Response prefixes every generated prompt and every generated expectations entry with [auto].
Response does NOT prefix name, id, expected_output, skill_name, skill_version, or description with [auto].
Response keeps files paths (when present) relative to the skill's eval directory, e.g. files/..., rather than absolute paths.

evals.name: generated-set-includes-negative-and-gradient
evals.prompt: Generate a focused, high-quality eval set for the commit-message-writer fixture. I care more about diverse coverage than volume.
evals.expected_output: A small but diverse set (roughly 4-8 evals) that follows the skill's design principles: specificity over breadth, at least one negative/When-NOT-to-use case, a difficulty gradient from an easy clear-cut scenario to a hard ambiguous one, and expectations that test understanding rather than keyword presence.
expectations: Response favors a focused set (roughly 4-8 diverse evals) over a large set of near-duplicate scenarios.
Response includes at least one negative eval derived from the When-NOT-to-use section (e.g. refusing to run git commit, changelog request, or no-changes input).
Response includes both an easy clear-cut scenario and a harder ambiguous or multi-concern scenario.
Response avoids expectations that pass for any answer mentioning a keyword, favoring checks on the actual conclusion or reasoning.

evals.name: eval-directory-layout-resolution
evals.prompt: Where will you write the generated evals.json for commit-message-writer, and how do you decide between the canonical location and a colocated one?
evals.expected_output: The skill should explain eval-directory resolution: FULL mode writes to the canonical evals/skills/{skill_name}/, INCREMENTAL mode writes back to whichever layout already holds the existing evals.json (canonical evals/skills/{skill_name}/ or colocated skills/{skill_name}/evals/), canonical wins if both exist, and it should not create a second copy that would make the runner warn about an ambiguous source.
expectations: Response states that a fresh FULL-mode generation is written to the canonical evals/skills/{skill_name}/ location.
Response states that INCREMENTAL mode writes back to whichever layout already contains the existing evals.json rather than creating a new one elsewhere.
Response identifies both the canonical evals/skills/{skill_name}/ and the colocated skills/{skill_name}/evals/ layouts and notes canonical wins if both already exist.
Response explains that creating a duplicate second copy is avoided because it would make the runner warn about an ambiguous eval source.
